enforcement_rules.single_responsibility: Each scene/script should have a single, well-defined responsibility
enforcement_rules.encapsulation: Keep internal state private and expose only necessary methods
enforcement_rules.autoload_minimization: Use autoloads sparingly, only for global singletons
enforcement_rules.node_hierarchy_depth: Keep scene tree depth reasonable (avoid excessive nesting)
enforcement_rules.resource_management: Use Resource types for data that doesn't need node functionality
enforcement_rules.signal_usage: Use signals for decoupled communication between nodes
enforcement_rules.script_organization: Keep scripts focused and under reasonable line limits
enforcement_rules.naming_conventions: Follow Godot naming conventions (snake_case for files, PascalCase for classes)

critical_enforcement.scene_tree_immutable: CRITICAL: Scene tree hierarchy, node names, and parent-child relationships are immutable. Modifying them destroys the project.
critical_enforcement.script_node_name_matching: CRITICAL: Script filenames must match their attached node names exactly. Mismatched names break script attachment.
critical_enforcement.script_paths_immutable: CRITICAL: Never move or rename existing script files. Godot stores script references by absolute path. Moving breaks all references.
critical_enforcement.autoload_configuration_immutable: CRITICAL: Never add, remove, or modify autoloads. Autoloads are global singletons loaded at startup. Modifying breaks global state.
critical_enforcement.folder_structure_immutable: CRITICAL: Never reorganize, rename, or move project folders. Godot uses relative paths extensively. Reorganization breaks all resource references.
critical_enforcement.resource_path_validation: CRITICAL: Validate all resource references (res:// paths) before use. Invalid paths cause load errors.
critical_enforcement.signal_connections_immutable: CRITICAL: Never modify or delete signal connections. Signals are the primary communication mechanism. Breaking them destroys node communication.
critical_enforcement.scene_files_read_only: CRITICAL: Never directly edit .tscn scene files. Scene files have complex structure. Direct editing corrupts scenes. Use Godot editor.

godot_mode_activation.trigger: Detection of project.godot file or .godot folder
godot_mode_activation.behavior: AI enters strict 'Godot Mode' with critical enforcement of project structure integrity
godot_mode_activation.failure_prevention: Prevents AI from breaking Godot projects by enforcing strict structure rules before any modification

common_ai_failures_prevented: Breaking scene trees by renaming nodes or changing hierarchy
Moving scripts and breaking all script references
Deleting or modifying autoloads and breaking global state
Reorganizing folders and breaking all resource paths
Directly editing .tscn files and corrupting scenes
Breaking signal connections and destroying node communication
Creating scripts with names that don't match nodes
Using unvalidated resource paths causing load errors
